{"step_by_step_thinking": "Non-paralytic polio refers to cases where the patient does not develop paralysis. Absent deep tendon reflexes, head drop, and nuchal rigidity are signs that can be associated with paralytic polio due to involvement of the spinal cord and brainstem. However, the knee heel test negative is a sign of lower motor neuron involvement, which can occur in non-paralytic polio as well. Among the given options, absent deep tendon reflexes (A) is more commonly associated with paralytic polio and thus would not be a feature of non-paralytic polio.", "answer_choice": "A"}
